differentials.name: Tumors from adjacent organs: pancreas, duodenum
differentials.raw: Tumors from adjacent organs: pancreas, duodenum

context: Location:   fundus (60%), body (30%), neck (10%) Growth types: √  mass replacing the gallbladder (37-70%) with engulfed gallstones √  irregular eccentric thickening of GB wall (20-30%) ← submucosal spread: √  focal (59%) / diffuse (41%) wall thickening DDx:  acute / chronic inflammation (usually < 10 mm) √  intraluminal polypoid / fungating "cauliflower-like" mass with wide base (15-25%) √  pericholecystic infiltration: in 76% focal, in 24% diffuse √  dilatation of biliary tree (38-70%): √  infiltrative tumor growth along cystic duct √  lymph node enlargement causing biliary obstruction √  int
